name: Circular Route 13 de Manteigas — Serra da Estrela
name_original: Percurso circular 13 de Manteigas — Serra da Estrela
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around Manteigas (São Pedro) are situated within the Serra da Estrela Natural Park, Portugal's largest protected natural area. The region is characterized by its prominent Zêzere Glacial Valley, offering routes with high peaks and forest-covered slopes. Cyclists encounter varied mountainous terrain, including granite boulders and significant elevation changes, alongside picturesque river valleys and glacial lakes. This landscape provides diverse challenges and scenic backdrops for touring cyclists. Best touring cycling routes around Manteigas (São Pedro) The most popular touring cycling route is Circular Route 13 de Manteigas — Serra da Estrela, a 19.6 miles (31.6 km) trail that takes 3 hours 3 minutes to complete. This difficult route features significant elevation gain and panoramic views of the Serra da Estrela. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Reboleira Meadow – Zêzere Glacial Valley loop from Sameiro, a difficult 19.9 miles (32.1 km) path. This route traverses the iconic U-shaped Zêzere Glacial Valley, showcasing its unique geological formations and forest-covered slopes. Local touring cyclists also love the Casa das Obras Fountain – Sabugueiro loop from Penhas da Saúde, a 48.3 miles (77.7 km) trail leading through high-altitude landscapes and mountain villages, often completed in about 6 hours 12 minutes. Touring cycling around Manteigas (São Pedro) is defined by glacial valleys, high-altitude lakes, and challenging mountainous terrain. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate paths to difficult ascents. The routes in Manteigas (São Pedro) are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.9 stars from over 10 reviews. More than 500 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Manteigas (São Pedro)'s varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Manteigas (São Pedro)?
A: There are over 45 touring cycling routes around Manteigas (São Pedro) listed on komoot. These routes offer a wide range of experiences within the Serra da Estrela Natural Park, catering to various skill levels.

Q: What is the general difficulty level of touring cycling routes in this region?
A: The touring cycling routes around Manteigas (São Pedro) are predominantly challenging, with 37 out of 45 routes rated as difficult. There are also 7 moderate routes and 1 easy option, ensuring there's something for every skill level, though experienced riders will find the most extensive network.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes in Manteigas (São Pedro)?
A: Yes, many routes are designed as loops, allowing you to start and end in the same location. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e2289772318/circular-route-13-de-manteigas-serra-da-estrela" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Circular Route 13 de Manteigas — Serra da Estrela&lt;/a&gt;, which offers panoramic views and a significant elevation gain over 31.6 km.

Q: What natural features can I expect to see along the touring cycling routes?
A: The routes traverse the stunning Zêzere Glacial Valley, a prominent U-shaped valley with high peaks and forest-covered slopes. You'll encounter diverse mountainous terrain, including granite boulders, and picturesque river valleys. Notable natural attractions include the glacial &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4915608" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Long Lake&lt;/a&gt; and the scenic Covão d'Ametade, where the Zêzere river flows through birch trees.

Q: Are there any waterfalls accessible by touring bike in the Manteigas area?
A: Yes, the region is home to several beautiful waterfalls. The famous Poço do Inferno (Hell's Well) is a stunning waterfall in a steep gorge, offering a refreshing stop. You can also find the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6019826" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hidden cascade at Poço do Inferno&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5904227" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Poço da Broca de Serapitel&lt;/a&gt;, which are popular highlights for cyclists.

Q: What kind of elevation changes should I prepare for on these routes?
A: Manteigas (São Pedro) is characterized by significant elevation changes due to its mountainous terrain within the Serra da Estrela Natural Park. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/21950240" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Casa das Obras Fountain – Sabugueiro loop from Penhas da Saúde&lt;/a&gt; involves over 2,200 meters of ascent, making it a challenging ride suitable for experienced touring cyclists.

Q: Are there any routes that showcase the Zêzere Glacial Valley specifically?
A: Absolutely. The Zêzere Glacial Valley is a central feature of the region. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/17314120" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Reboleira Meadow – Zêzere Glacial Valley loop from Sameiro&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 32.1 km path that traverses this iconic U-shaped valley, allowing you to experience its unique geological formations and forest-covered slopes firsthand.

Q: What do other touring cyclists say about the routes in Manteigas (São Pedro)?
A: The touring cycling routes in Manteigas (São Pedro) are highly regarded by the komoot community, with an average rating of 4.9 out of 5 stars from over 10 reviews. Cyclists often praise the dramatic glacial valleys, challenging mountain passes, and stunning natural park scenery.

Q: Are there any cultural or historical sites to explore along the cycling routes?
A: While primarily focused on nature, Manteigas offers cultural points of interest. The village itself is known for traditional factories, such as those producing Burel fabric. You might also encounter historical sites like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2626824" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Casa do Juiz (Nave da Mestra)&lt;/a&gt;, adding a unique dimension to your tour.

Q: Can I find routes that lead to the highest point in mainland Portugal?
A: Yes, some routes in the Serra da Estrela Natural Park can lead towards Torre, the highest point in mainland Portugal at 1,993 meters. While challenging, reaching &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1258525" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Torre (Serra da Estrela Summit)&lt;/a&gt; offers incredibly rewarding views. An example of a long, challenging route in the area is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10370545" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Serra da Estrela – Covão d'Ametade loop from Manteigas&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the best time of year for touring cycling in Manteigas (São Pedro)?
A: The region is suitable for cycling from spring through autumn. Spring brings blooming landscapes, while autumn offers enchanting pastoral scenery, particularly around the beeches of São Lourenço. Summers are generally warm, and while winter cycling is possible, higher altitudes can experience snow and colder conditions.

Q: Are there any family-friendly touring cycling options in Manteigas (São Pedro)?
A: While most routes are rated difficult, there is at least one easy touring cycling route available. For families, it's advisable to look for routes with minimal elevation gain and shorter distances, often found closer to the village or along flatter valley sections, though these are less common in this mountainous region.

Q: Where can I park my car when starting a touring cycling route in Manteigas?
A: Parking is generally available in and around the village of Manteigas. For specific routes, you might find designated parking areas. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/32176370" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bike loop from Estacionamento do Mondeguinho&lt;/a&gt; suggests a starting point with parking facilities, but it's always good to check local signage upon arrival.
tips: It is possible to rely only on a mat and a sleeping bag to sleep here and continue traveling the next morning. This building has a sheltered porch. It serves well, if the weather makes it easier.
